query: Paris court verdict cyberbullying Brigitte Macron number people found guilty

{"query":"Paris court verdict cyberbullying Brigitte Macron number people found guilty","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1214,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.france24.com/en/video/20260105-ten-people-found-guilty-of-cyberbullying-france-s-first-lady-brigitte-macron","title":"Ten people found guilty of cyberbullying France's first lady Brigitte Macron - France 24","content":"The court convicted all defendants to sentences ranging from a cyberbullying awareness training to 8-month suspended prison sentences."},{"url":"https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/paris-court-finds-10-guilty-of-cyberbullying-french-first-lady-brigitte-macron","title":"Paris court finds 10 guilty of cyberbullying French first lady Brigitte Macron | PBS News","content":"All 10 were ordered to attend cyberbullying awareness training.
The X account of Aurélien Poirson-Atlan, 41, known as Zoé Sagan on social media, was suspended in 2024 after his name was cited in several judicial investigations.
Poirson-Atlan was given an eight-month prison sentence, along with another defendant, a gallery owner.
All 10 were also sentenced to jointly pay 10,000 euros ($11,675) in compensation to Brigitte Macron for moral damage.
Emmanuel Macron, 48, has been France's president since 2017."},{"url":"https://www.dw.com/en/france-10-found-guilty-of-cyberbullying-brigitte-macron/a-75392821","title":"France: 10 found guilty of cyberbullying Brigitte Macron","content":"Tiphaine Auziere, Macron's daughter from a previous marriage, told the court that her mother "cannot ignore the horrible things" people post about her on the internet, adding that the posts also affected the French First Lady's grandchildren.
The Macrons also pressed charges in the US in July 2025 against Candace Owens, a conservative internet influencer, for an alleged "campaign of global humiliation" against the couple due to promoting the conspiracy that Brigitte Macron was born a man."},{"url":"https://www.france24.com/en/france/20260105-ten-people-found-guilty-cyberbullying-france-first-lady-brigitte-macron","title":"Ten people found guilty of cyberbullying France’s First Lady Brigitte Macron - France 24","content":"The court pointed to “particularly degrading, insulting, and malicious” comments referring to false claims regarding alleged trans identity and alleged pedocriminality targeting Brigitte Macron.
A Paris court on Monday found ten people guilty of cyberbullying France’s First Lady Brigitte Macron by spreading false online claims about her gender and sexuality, including allegations she was born a man.
The defendants, eight men and two women aged 41 to 65, are accused of having posted “numerous malicious comments” falsely claiming that President Emmanuel Macron’s wife was born a man and linking their 24-year age gap to paedophilia.
Her daughter, Tiphaine Auzière, testified about what she described as the “deterioration” of her mother’s life since the online harassment intensified.
She said the impact has extended to the entire family, including Macron’s grandchildren.
Brigitte Macron, 24 years her husband’s senior, was then called Brigitte Auzière, a married mother of three."},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/paris-court-finds-10-people-guilty-of-cyberbullying-brigitte-macron-over-trans-conspiracy/","title":"Paris court finds 10 people guilty of cyberbullying Brigitte Macron over trans conspiracy | Courthouse News Service","content":"Paris court finds 10 people guilty of cyberbullying Brigitte Macron over trans conspiracy
French President Emmanuel Macron and his wife are involved in multiple legal battles to fight claims that France's first lady is actually a man.
Ten people — eight men and two women between the ages of 41 and 60 — went on trial for harassing Brigitte Macron online, specifically about her gender.
The online accounts of self-proclaimed medium Amandine Roy and influencer Zoé Sagan will also be suspended for six months.
Although there were rumors about her gender identify when Macron became president in 2017, the story exploded in 2021 when Delphine Jégousse, the self-proclaimed medium and journalist known as Amandine Roy, posted a four-hour video on her YouTube channel interviewing Christelle L., another self-proclaimed journalist known as Natacha Rey."}]}
